description: Represents the number one. It's a fundamental radical and component in many other kanji, used for counting, dates (like the first day), and indicating 'one of something'.
mnemonic: A single horizontal stroke — imagine the horizon line, the very first mark of counting.
radical: Radical: 一 (one) — the simplest radical, a lone brushstroke.
stroke_hint: One left-to-right horizontal stroke.

examples.meaning_mm: တစ်ယောက်တည်း

description: Represents the number two. Commonly used in counting, for the month of February (二月), and in various compound words.
mnemonic: Two horizontal lines stacked — like two tally marks counting up to 2.
radical: Radical: 二 (two) — two parallel horizontal lines.
stroke_hint: Top horizontal stroke first, then the longer bottom stroke.

examples.meaning_mm: ဖေဖော်ဝါရီလ

description: Represents the number three. Used for counting, dates (the third day), the month of March (三月), and in words like 'triangle' (三角形).
kunyomi: み, み.つ, みっ.つ
mnemonic: Three horizontal lines — like three tally marks or stripes on a uniform.
radical: Radical: 三 (three) — three stacked horizontal lines.
stroke_hint: Top, middle, then bottom horizontal strokes, each slightly longer.

description: Represents the number four. It has two common readings, 'yon' and 'shi'. The reading 'shi' is sometimes avoided as it has the same sound as the word for death (死).
kunyomi: よ, よ.つ, よっ.つ, よん
mnemonic: A box with two legs at the bottom — like a table resting on four legs.
radical: Radical: 囗 (enclosure) + legs — a box enclosing an interior divided by two strokes.
stroke_hint: Outer box first (left, top, right), then inner cross, then close bottom, finally two leg strokes.

description: Represents the number five. Used in counting, time (five o'clock), the month of May (五月), and money.
mnemonic: A cross shape with a hook and a top bar — think of a crooked Roman numeral V with a lid.
radical: Radical: 二 (two) + 乙 (second) — multiple elements suggesting 'the fifth step.'
stroke_hint: Top horizontal, then the down-left diagonal, then horizontal, then vertical with hook.

description: Represents the number six. It's used in counting, for dates like the sixth day of the month (六日, muika), and for the month of June (六月).
kunyomi: む, む.つ, むっ.つ, むい
mnemonic: A small roof with two diverging legs — like a little house with two feet walking away.
radical: Radical: 亠 (lid/roof) + 儿 (legs) — a cover on top of two feet.
stroke_hint: Dot, top horizontal, then left leg stroke, then right leg stroke.

description: Represents the number seven. It has two common readings, 'nana' and 'shichi'. Used for the month of July (七月) and for the Tanabata festival (七夕).
kunyomi: なな, なな.つ, なの
mnemonic: A horizontal stroke with a curve going down-left — looks like the number 7 written in Japanese style.
radical: Radical: 一 (one) + 乙 (second/twist) — a straight line with a twist.
stroke_hint: Horizontal stroke first, then a curving stroke going down and to the left.

description: Represents the number eight. Its shape is considered lucky because it widens at the bottom, suggesting growing prosperity. Used for the month of August (八月).
kunyomi: や, や.つ, やっ.つ, よう
mnemonic: Two diverging strokes opening outward — like two arms spread wide saying 'this much!'
radical: Radical: 八 (eight) — two diverging strokes, the base radical itself.
stroke_hint: Left diagonal stroke going down-left, then right diagonal stroke going down-right.

description: Represents the number nine. The reading 'ku' is sometimes avoided as it sounds like the word for suffering (苦), so 'kyuu' is often used instead. Used for the month of September (九月).
mnemonic: A horizontal stroke and a curved hook — like the number 9, a hook reaching back on itself.
radical: Radical: 乙 (second/bent) — a bent line suggesting going back around.
stroke_hint: Horizontal stroke first, then a long curving stroke that turns and hooks upward.

description: Represents the number ten. This kanji is a fundamental building block for larger numbers, such as eleven (十一) and one hundred (百). Used for the month of October (十月).
mnemonic: A perfect cross — horizontal meets vertical at the center, simple and complete.
radical: Radical: 十 (ten) — the cross shape itself is the radical.
stroke_hint: Horizontal stroke first, then vertical stroke crossing through its center.

examples.meaning_mm: အောက်တိုဘာလ

description: Represents the number one hundred. The pronunciation can change depending on the number preceding it (e.g., 三百 is 'sanbyaku', 六百 is 'roppyaku').
mnemonic: The number one (一) on top of white (白) — imagine a perfect white score of 100.
radical: Radical: 白 (white) + 一 (one) — one above white: a pristine hundred.
stroke_hint: Top horizontal stroke (一), then the short vertical, then enclosing box strokes for 白.

examples.meaning_mm: ဂျပန်ငွေ ၁၀၀ ယင်း

description: Represents the number one thousand. Like 'hundred', its pronunciation can also change with certain numbers (e.g., 三千 is 'sanzen').
mnemonic: A person (人) walking under a horizontal line — someone striding forward into the thousands.
radical: Radical: 十 (ten) + ノ (diagonal stroke) — ten with a sweeping stroke, suggesting great multitude.
stroke_hint: Diagonal stroke first (top-left to bottom-right), then the cross (horizontal then vertical).

description: Represents ten thousand. This is a primary unit for counting large numbers in Japanese. For example, 100,000 is written as 十万 (ten ten-thousands).
mnemonic: Wide sweeping strokes suggesting vastness — like a horizon stretching ten thousand miles.
radical: Radical: 艸 (grass variant) — originally depicted a scorpion, now used for 'ten thousand.'
stroke_hint: First stroke horizontal, then a short vertical, then curving long stroke to the right and down.

examples.meaning_mm: ဂျပန်ငွေ တစ်သောင်း ယင်း
ဖောင်တိန်းပင်

description: This kanji means 'yen', the currency of Japan. It can also mean 'circle' or 'round', and is read as 'maru' in that context.
meaning_mm: ယန်း၊ စက်ဝိုင်း
mnemonic: A box with a horizontal divider and legs — like a coin (circle) caught in a rectangular frame.
radical: Radical: 囗 (enclosure) + 人 (person) variant — something enclosed, like a coin in a wallet.
stroke_hint: Outer enclosure (left vertical, top horizontal, right vertical), then inner stroke, then bottom close.

examples.meaning_mm: ဂျပန်ငွေ ၁၀၀ ယင်း

description: A very common kanji for 'person'. The reading changes based on context: 'hito' when used alone, 'jin' for nationalities (e.g., 日本人 - nihonjin), and 'nin' for counting people (e.g., 三人 - sannin).
mnemonic: Two legs walking — a stick figure seen from the side, mid-stride.
radical: Radical: 人 (person) — the most fundamental human radical.
stroke_hint: Left diagonal stroke going down-left, then right diagonal stroke going down-right from near the top.

examples.meaning_mm: ဂျပန်လူမျိုး

description: A core kanji meaning 'sun', 'day', or 'Japan'. It's used in days of the week (日曜日 - Sunday), dates (e.g., 26日 - 26th day), and in the name of Japan (日本 - Nihon).
meaning_mm: နေ့၊ နေ၊ ဂျပန်
mnemonic: A rectangle with a horizontal line through the middle — like a window with a sunbeam divider.
radical: Radical: 日 (sun/day) — the sun radical, one of the most common in Japanese.
stroke_hint: Outer box (left, top, right), then middle horizontal stroke, then close the bottom.

examples.meaning_mm: တနင်္ဂနွေနေ့

description: This kanji means 'moon' or 'month'. It is used for Monday (月曜日 - getsuyoubi) and to count months (e.g., 九月 - kugatsu, September).
mnemonic: A crescent moon in a frame — like catching a new moon inside a rectangular border.
radical: Radical: 月 (moon/month) — the moon radical, also means 'month.'
stroke_hint: Left vertical, top curving to right, then two horizontal strokes inside, then short right vertical.

examples.meaning_mm: တနင်္လာနေ့

description: Represents 'fire'. It is the first kanji in the word for Tuesday (火曜日 - kayoubi).
mnemonic: A person with arms spread at the bottom and a flame rising above — like someone standing near a bonfire.
radical: Radical: 火 (fire) — the fire radical.
stroke_hint: Left diagonal, then right diagonal (the top), then left leg, then right leg at the bottom.

description: Represents 'water'. It is used in the word for Wednesday (水曜日 - suiyoubi).
mnemonic: A central stroke with water flowing on both sides — like a river with currents diverging from the center.
radical: Radical: 水 (water) — the water radical.
stroke_hint: Center vertical with a dot, then left diagonal strokes flowing outward, then right strokes.

examples.meaning_mm: ဗုဒ္ဓဟူးနေ့
ရေကူးခြင်း

description: Represents 'tree' or 'wood'. It is used in the word for Thursday (木曜日 - mokuyoubi).
meaning_mm: သစ်ပင်၊ သစ်သား
mnemonic: A tree trunk with branches spreading up and roots at the bottom — like a simple tree diagram.
radical: Radical: 木 (tree/wood) — the wood/tree radical.
stroke_hint: Horizontal stroke, then vertical through its center, then left diagonal root, then right diagonal root.

examples.meaning_mm: ကြာသပတေးနေ့
ကိမုရ (မျိုးနွယ်နာမည်)

description: Represents 'gold', 'metal', and by extension, 'money'. It is used in the word for Friday (金曜日 - kinyoubi) and money (お金 - okane).
mnemonic: A roof covering two rows of treasures, with small dots — like a safe full of gold coins.
radical: Radical: 金 (gold/metal) — the metal radical, used in words about money and metals.
stroke_hint: Roof strokes, then main vertical, then two horizontal strokes inside, then two small dot strokes.

description: Represents 'earth', 'soil', or 'ground'. It is used in the word for Saturday (土曜日 - doyoubi).
mnemonic: A cross planted in the ground on a base line — like a marker staked into the earth.
radical: Radical: 土 (earth/soil) — the earth radical, found in many place-related kanji.
stroke_hint: Top short horizontal stroke, then the center vertical downward, then the longer bottom horizontal.

description: This kanji is used as a suffix for the days of the week, as in 月曜日 (getsuyoubi - Monday). It does not typically appear on its own.
meaning_mm: ရက်သတ္တပတ်၏နေ့
mnemonic: The sun (日) with a bird's wing (羽) — like the sun flying through each day of the week.
radical: Radical: 日 (sun) — the sun radical on the left side.
stroke_hint: Write 日 on left, then 隹 in center, then 羽 (wing) strokes on right.

examples.meaning_mm: တနင်္လာနေ့

description: Means 'up', 'above', 'on', or 'top'. It is the opposite of 下 (shita - down). Can also be part of verbs like 上がる (agaru - to go up).
kunyomi: うえ, あ.がる, のぼ.る
meaning_mm: အပေါ်၊ အထက်
mnemonic: An arrow pointing up above a base line — like marking 'above' on a diagram.
radical: Radical: 上 (up/above) — itself a directional radical.
stroke_hint: Short vertical above a horizontal base, then a longer horizontal below.

description: Means 'down', 'below', 'under', or 'bottom'. It is the opposite of 上 (ue - up). Can also be part of verbs like 下がる (sagaru - to go down).
kunyomi: した, さ.がる, くだ.る
meaning_mm: အောက်၊ အောက်တွင်
mnemonic: A line above with a downward point — like an arrow pointing downward below a shelf.
radical: Radical: 下 (down/below) — itself a directional radical.
stroke_hint: Horizontal stroke first, then short vertical from the middle, then a small dot below.

description: Means 'middle', 'center', or 'inside'. Used in words like China (中国 - Chuugoku) and 'all day long' (一日中 - ichinichijuu).
meaning_mm: အလယ်၊ အတွင်း
mnemonic: A rectangle with a line straight through the center — like an arrow hitting the bullseye.
radical: Radical: 囗 (enclosure) + 中 — a line piercing through a box, hitting the center.
stroke_hint: Vertical stroke first piercing the box, then the outer box (left, top, right, bottom).

examples.meaning_mm: အလယ်တန်းကျောင်း
တရုတ်နိုင်ငံ

description: Means 'half'. It is commonly used when telling time, for example, 二時半 (niji han) means 'half past two' or 2:30.
mnemonic: Four strokes balanced over a cross (十) — imagine cutting the number ten exactly in half.
radical: Radical: 十 (ten) — a cross with a dividing stroke, showing division.
stroke_hint: Top left dot, top right dot, then horizontal stroke, then vertical cross stroke.

examples.meaning_mm: ၁ နာရီ ထက်ဝက်

description: Represents 'year'. It is used to count years (e.g., 2025年) and in words like 'last year' (去年 - kyonen) and 'this year' (今年 - kotoshi).
mnemonic: A grain stalk bending with the seasons — the yearly harvest represented by a bending stalk.
radical: Radical: 禾 (grain) variant — growth through a full year cycle.
stroke_hint: Top strokes form a cross/T shape, then three downward strokes suggesting a stalk bending.

description: Means 'time' or 'hour'. Used as a counter for the hour (e.g., 一時 - ichiji, one o'clock). Also found in the word 'time' (時間 - jikan).
meaning_mm: အချိန်၊ နာရီ
mnemonic: The sun (日) passing by a Buddhist temple (寺) — the temple bells mark the passing hours.
radical: Radical: 日 (sun) + 寺 (temple) — sun at the temple tower, marking time.
stroke_hint: Write 日 on left, then 土 on top right, then 寸 on bottom right.

examples.meaning_mm: ဘယ်နှစ်နာရီ

description: A versatile kanji meaning 'minute' (fun/pun), 'to divide' (wakeru), or 'to understand' (wakaru). In the context of time, 一分 is 'ippun'.
onyomi: フン, ブン, プン
meaning_mm: မိနစ်၊ အပိုင်း၊ နားလည်သည်
mnemonic: A sword (刀) dividing something — like a knife splitting time into minutes and parts.
radical: Radical: 刀 (sword/knife) + 八 (eight/divide) — a blade separating things.
stroke_hint: Top left diagonal, top right diagonal (八), then left diagonal of blade, then right hook stroke.

examples.meaning_mm: မိမိကိုယ်တိုင်

description: The essential question word for 'what'. It is read as 'nan' before certain counters (e.g., 何時 - nanji, what time?) and 'nani' in other cases (e.g., 何ですか - nani desu ka, what is it?).
mnemonic: A person (亻) carrying a burden and asking — 'what is this heavy thing I'm carrying?'
radical: Radical: 亻 (person) — a person on the left side.
stroke_hint: Person radical on left (two strokes), then the right side 可 (can/permitted).

examples.meaning_mm: ဘယ်နှစ်နာရီ

description: The standard, polite word for 'I' or 'me'. It can also mean 'private' in some contexts, like 私立 (shiritsu - private establishment).
meaning_mm: ကျွန်တော်/ကျွန်မ၊ ပုဂ္ဂလိက
mnemonic: A grain stalk (禾) next to a private mark (ム) — the farmer claiming 'this crop is mine, private property.'
radical: Radical: 禾 (grain/rice plant) — on the left, a rice stalk symbolizing personal ownership.
stroke_hint: Write 禾 on left, then ム (two curved strokes) on the right.

examples.meaning_mm: ပုဂ္ဂလိကပိုင်

description: Means 'father'. Read as 'chichi' when referring to your own father and お父さん (otousan) when addressing him or talking about someone else's father.
mnemonic: Like two crossed sticks with a downward stroke — imagine a father crossing his tools at the end of a work day.
radical: Radical: 父 (father) — the father radical itself, found in paternal relationship words.
stroke_hint: Two diagonal crossing strokes at top, then left downward stroke, then right downward stroke.

description: Means 'mother'. Read as 'haha' when referring to your own mother and お母さん (okaasan) when addressing her or talking about someone else's mother.
mnemonic: A woman figure with two dots of milk — like a nursing mother's silhouette.
radical: Radical: 女 (woman) variant — the woman radical modified to show nurturing.
stroke_hint: Central vertical, left horizontal, right horizontal, then two inner dot strokes, then bottom.

description: Means 'child'. It's often seen in words like 'child' (子供 - kodomo), 'boy' (男の子 - otoko no ko) and 'girl' (女の子 - onna no ko).
mnemonic: A swaddled baby with one arm reaching up — an infant seeking attention.
radical: Radical: 子 (child) — the child radical, found in many words about children and learning.
stroke_hint: Curved stroke on top forming head and arm, then horizontal stroke, then short bottom stroke.

description: Means 'man' or 'male'. Found in words like 'man' (男の人 - otoko no hito) and 'boy' (男の子 - otoko no ko).
mnemonic: A rice field (田) with strength (力) below — a man uses his strength to work the fields.
radical: Radical: 田 (rice field) + 力 (strength) — combining agriculture and physical power.
stroke_hint: Write 田 (grid square) on top, then 力 (strength, two strokes) below.

description: Means 'woman' or 'female'. Found in words like 'woman' (女の人 - onna no hito) and 'girl' (女の子 - onna no ko).
meaning_mm: မိန်းမ၊ အမ
mnemonic: Like a woman seated with legs crossed and arms extended gracefully to each side.
radical: Radical: 女 (woman) — one of the most common radicals, found in many character-related words.
stroke_hint: Left diagonal stroke, then crossing horizontal stroke, then right diagonal stroke downward.

description: Means 'mountain'. A very common kanji found in names and geographical terms. For example, Mt. Fuji is 富士山 (Fujisan).
mnemonic: Three peaks with the center tallest — exactly what a mountain range looks like on the horizon.
radical: Radical: 山 (mountain) — the mountain radical, one of the most visually recognizable.
stroke_hint: Left shorter vertical, then taller center vertical, then right shorter vertical, all on a base.

examples.meaning_mm: ယာမာဒါ (မျိုးနွယ်နာမည်)
ဖူဂျိ တောင်

description: Means 'river'. The reading can change to '-gawa' when it's the second part of a compound word, like in 中川 (Nakagawa), a common surname.
mnemonic: Three vertical lines flowing side by side — like three streams forming a river.
radical: Radical: 川 (river) — the river radical, flowing downward.
stroke_hint: Left short vertical, then middle taller vertical, then right vertical curving slightly.

description: Means 'rice paddy' or 'rice field'. This is an extremely common kanji in Japanese surnames, such as 山田 (Yamada) and 田中 (Tanaka). The reading often changes to '-da'.
mnemonic: A grid divided into four sections — a bird's-eye view of Japanese rice paddies.
radical: Radical: 田 (rice field) — the field radical, found in agriculture-related kanji.
stroke_hint: Outer box first (left, top, right, bottom), then vertical divider, then horizontal divider.

examples.meaning_mm: ယာမာဒါ (မျိုးနွယ်နာမည်)

description: A versatile kanji meaning 'life', 'to live' (生きる - ikiru), or 'to be born' (生まれる - umareru). It's a key part of words like 'student' (学生 - gakusei) and 'teacher' (先生 - sensei).
kunyomi: い.きる, う.まれる, なま
meaning: life, to live, to be born, student
meaning_mm: ဘဝ၊ အသက်ရှင်သည်၊ မွေးဖွားသည်၊ ကျောင်းသား
mnemonic: A seedling pushing up through the soil — a small sprout representing the birth of life.
radical: Radical: 生 (life/birth) — the life radical, appearing in words about living and birth.
stroke_hint: Short left stroke on top, then horizontal, then longer horizontal, then vertical going down.

description: Means 'ahead', 'previous', or 'before'. It combines with 生 to form 'teacher' (先生 - sensei, literally 'one born before'). Also used for 'last week' (先週 - senshuu).
meaning_mm: ရှေ့၊ အရင်၊ ဦးစွာ
mnemonic: Someone walking ahead with legs (儿) — the front runner going before everyone else.
radical: Radical: 儿 (legs/person at bottom) — legs moving forward, suggesting precedence.
stroke_hint: Top portion (土-like shape), then two leg strokes at the bottom.

examples.meaning_mm: အပတ်ကတည်းက

description: Relates to 'learning', 'study', or 'school'. It combines with 生 to mean 'student' (学生 - gakusei) and with 校 for 'school' (学校 - gakkou).
meaning_mm: သင်ယူသည်၊ လေ့လာသည်၊ ကျောင်း
mnemonic: A child (子) reaching up under a roof toward a shape representing learning — knowledge descending to a student.
radical: Radical: 子 (child) — at the bottom, a child striving to learn.
stroke_hint: Top strokes form crossed hands reaching (爻), then 冖 cover, then 子 at bottom.

description: Means 'school' or 'exam'. It is most often seen combined with 学 to form the word for school (学校 - gakkou).
mnemonic: A tree (木) with a crossing pattern — like a tree branching in structured directions, symbolizing organized learning.
radical: Radical: 木 (tree/wood) — on the left, wood representing a structured building.
stroke_hint: Write 木 on left, then 交 (cross/mix) on right.

examples.meaning_mm: အထက်တန်းကျောင်း

description: Means 'friend'. The most common word using this kanji is 友達 (tomodachi).
meaning_mm: သူငယ်ချင်း
mnemonic: Like a right hand reaching forward — two friends reaching their right hands toward each other.
radical: Radical: 又 (right hand/again) — the hand radical showing friendship through a handshake.
stroke_hint: Top left horizontal, then short stroke, then the bent right-hand shape (又).

examples.meaning_mm: သူငယ်ချင်း
ရင်းနှီးသော သူငယ်ချင်း

description: Means 'book', 'origin', or 'main'. It is also used as a counter for long, cylindrical objects. It is the 'hon' in Nihon (日本 - Japan).
meaning_mm: စာအုပ်၊ မူလ၊ အဓိက
mnemonic: A tree (木) with a mark at the base highlighting the root — the 'origin' or 'main' part of something.
radical: Radical: 木 (tree) — the tree with an extra stroke marking the root/base.
stroke_hint: Write 木, then add a short horizontal stroke at the root (below the main horizontal).

examples.meaning_mm: ဂျပန်နိုင်ငံ

description: Means 'every'. It's used as a prefix in time-related words like 'every day' (毎日 - mainichi), 'every week' (毎週 - maishuu), and 'every year' (毎年 - maitoshi/mainen).
mnemonic: A shape like 'mother' (母) with an added stroke — like a mother who attends to things 'every' day without fail.
radical: Radical: 母 (mother) variant — maternal consistency suggesting daily repetition.
stroke_hint: Top cap stroke, then left and right cross strokes, then central vertical with inner strokes.

examples.meaning_mm: မနက်တိုင်း

description: Means 'language' or 'word'. It's used as a suffix for languages, such as Japanese (日本語 - Nihongo) and English (英語 - Eigo).
meaning_mm: ဘာသာစကား၊ စကားလုံး
mnemonic: Speech (言) combined with 'I/myself' (吾) — 'my speech' means language and words.
radical: Radical: 言 (speech/say) — on the left, words coming from a mouth.
stroke_hint: Write 言 on left, then 吾 (五 over 口) on right.

examples.meaning_mm: အင်္ဂလိပ်ဘာသာ

description: Relates to 'sentence', 'writing', or 'literature'. Used in words like 'grammar' (文法 - bunpou) and 'literature' (文学 - bungaku).
meaning_mm: ဝါကျ၊ စာပေ၊ အရေးအသား
mnemonic: Like a person with crossed legs and flowing brushwork — the artistic shape of writing letters.
radical: Radical: 文 (writing/sentence) — itself a radical representing letters and patterns.
stroke_hint: Top dot, then the crossing diagonal strokes, creating an X-like pattern with a base.

description: A fundamental verb meaning 'to go' (行く - iku). It is also read as 'kou' in compound words like 'bank' (銀行 - ginkou) or 'gyou' for 'line' or 'row'.
mnemonic: A crossroad with footsteps — like feet walking down a road at an intersection.
radical: Radical: 行 (go/conduct) — the go radical, found in movement-related words.
stroke_hint: Left side: two short vertical strokes; then right side: three strokes forming a similar shape.

description: A fundamental irregular verb meaning 'to come' (来る - kuru). The 'on' reading 'rai' is used in time words like 'next week' (来週 - raishuu) and 'next month' (来月 - raigetsu).
meaning_mm: လာသည်၊ နောက်
mnemonic: A tree (木) with extra strokes suggesting arms reaching — someone coming toward you from behind a tree.
radical: Radical: 木 (tree) — a tree with additional coming-motion strokes.
stroke_hint: Top horizontal, then diagonal strokes (like wings), then center vertical, then lower diagonals.

description: The verb 'to see', 'to look', or 'to watch' (見る - miru). It is also part of the verb 'to show' (見せる - miseru).
meaning_mm: ကြည့်သည်၊ မြင်သည်
mnemonic: An eye (目) on two legs — like an eye with legs walking around seeing everything.
radical: Radical: 見 (see/look) — the see radical, with an eye on top of legs.
stroke_hint: Write 目 (eye box with horizontal strokes) on top, then two leg strokes (儿) at bottom.

description: Means 'to eat' (食べる - taberu). The 'on' reading 'shoku' is used in words like 'cafeteria' (食堂 - shokudou) and 'food' (食べ物 - tabemono).
meaning_mm: စားသည်၊ အစားအစာ
mnemonic: A pyramid/roof above a round pot — like steam rising from a covered cooking pot full of food.
radical: Radical: 食 (eat/food) — the eat radical, found in all food-related kanji.
stroke_hint: Top inverted-V shape, then right angle strokes, then a rounded bottom portion.

examples.meaning_mm: မုန့်ဆိုင်

description: Means 'to drink' (飲む - nomu). It is used in compound words like 'beverage' (飲み物 - nomimono) and 'drinking and eating' (飲食 - inshoku).
mnemonic: Food (食) on the left, and a figure opening its mouth wide on the right — tilting the head back to drink.
radical: Radical: 食 (eat) — on the left, the eating/food radical.
stroke_hint: Write 食 on left, then 欠 (yawning person) on right — mouth open wide.

description: Means 'to buy' (買う - kau). A common related word is 'shopping' (買い物 - kaimono).
mnemonic: A net (罒) catching shellfish (貝) — in ancient Japan, shells were used as currency, so catching them meant buying.
radical: Radical: 貝 (shellfish/money) — shells historically used as currency.
stroke_hint: Top horizontal strokes forming a net shape (罒), then the shellfish shape (貝) below.

examples.meaning_mm: ဈေးဝယ်ခြင်း

description: A common adjective meaning 'high', 'tall', or 'expensive' (高い - takai). Also used in words like 'high school' (高校 - koukou).
meaning_mm: မြင့်သည်၊ အရပ်ရှည်သည်၊ ဈေးကြီးသည်
mnemonic: A tall pagoda tower with a window — like a Japanese temple reaching high into the sky.
radical: Radical: 高 (high/tall) — itself a radical for height-related words.
stroke_hint: Top dotted roof strokes, then rectangular window shape, then mouth shape, then wide base.

examples.meaning_mm: အထက်တန်းကျောင်း

description: Means 'cheap' or 'inexpensive' (安い - yasui). It can also mean 'peaceful' or 'safe', as in the word 安全 (anzen - safety).
mnemonic: A woman (女) safe under a roof (宀) — a woman sheltered at home brings peace and safety.
radical: Radical: 宀 (roof) + 女 (woman) — shelter over woman meaning safe and cheap.
stroke_hint: Roof strokes first (宀), then the woman (女) strokes below.

description: Means 'big' or 'large' (大きい - ookii). The 'on' reading 'dai' is used in words like 'university' (大学 - daigaku) and 'important' (大事 - daiji).
mnemonic: A person with arms spread wide — like a person saying 'this BIG!' with outstretched arms.
radical: Radical: 大 (big/large) — the large radical, one of the most common.
stroke_hint: Horizontal stroke through center, then left diagonal going down-left, then right diagonal down-right.

description: Means 'small' or 'little' (小さい - chiisai). The 'on' reading 'shou' is used in words like 'elementary school' (小学校 - shougakkou).
kunyomi: ちい.さい, こ, お
mnemonic: A central vertical with two tiny dots on either side — one big line flanked by two tiny specks.
radical: Radical: 小 (small) — the small radical, representing diminutive size.
stroke_hint: Center vertical stroke first, then left downward dot/stroke, then right downward dot/stroke.

examples.meaning_mm: မူလတန်းကျောင်း

description: Means 'new' (新しい - atarashii). The 'on' reading 'shin' is used in words like 'newspaper' (新聞 - shinbun) and 'Shinkansen' (新幹線 - bullet train).
mnemonic: A person standing (立) next to a tree (木) with an ax (斤) — freshly chopped wood is brand new.
radical: Radical: 斤 (ax) + 木 (tree) + 立 (stand) — cutting a new tree with an ax.
stroke_hint: Left side: 立 then 木 stacked; right side: 斤 (ax shape).

examples.meaning_mm: မြင်းစိုင်းရထား

description: Means 'right' (direction). Used in words like 右手 (migite - right hand) and 右側 (migigawa - right side). The opposite is 左 (hidari - left).
mnemonic: A hand stroke above a mouth (口) — your right hand raised to answer a question using your mouth.
radical: Radical: 口 (mouth) + 又 (right hand) — using the right hand alongside speech.
stroke_hint: Top-left stroke of hand first, then the horizontal, then the mouth box (口) below.

description: Means 'left' (direction). Used in words like 左手 (hidarite - left hand) and 左側 (hidarigawa - left side). The opposite is 右 (migi - right).
mnemonic: A left-hand stroke above the work (工) — the left hand doing skilled craft work.
radical: Radical: 工 (work/craft) — work done with the left hand.
stroke_hint: Top-left stroke first, then horizontal stroke over 工 (top horizontal, vertical, bottom horizontal).

description: Means 'north'. Used in geographical terms like 北海道 (Hokkaidou) and compass directions. Part of the word 北口 (kitaguchi - north exit).
mnemonic: Two people standing back-to-back — in the cold north, two people turn their backs to the freezing wind.
radical: Radical: 匕 (spoon/person variant) — two figures facing away from each other.
stroke_hint: Left vertical stroke, then short horizontal, then right side: diagonal down and short horizontal.

description: Means 'south'. Used in geographical terms and compass directions. Part of the word 南口 (minamiguchi - south exit).
mnemonic: Like a tent structure with a hanging cord — the south is warm, things hang loosely in the heat.
radical: Radical: 十 (ten) + 冂 (frame) + interior strokes — a structured enclosure with hanging elements.
stroke_hint: Top cross shape, then frame shape, then inner horizontal and vertical strokes.

examples.meaning_mm: တောင်ဘက် ထွက်ပေါက်

description: Means 'east'. The 'on' reading 'tou' is used in words like 東京 (Toukyou - Tokyo, literally 'Eastern Capital') and 東口 (higashiguchi - east exit).
mnemonic: The sun (日) rising up behind a tree (木) — the sun appears in the east behind the treetops.
radical: Radical: 木 (tree) + 日 (sun) — sun and tree combined to point east.
stroke_hint: Top horizontal, then 日 (sun box) in the middle of a vertical, then lower diagonal strokes like roots.

description: Means 'west'. Used in geographical terms like 西口 (nishiguchi - west exit) and in proper nouns. The opposite of 東 (higashi - east).
mnemonic: Like a bird perching in a nest — birds return to their nests in the west as the evening sun sets.
radical: Radical: 覀 (west variant) — a bird settling into a nest, westernward.
stroke_hint: Top horizontal, then left and right vertical strokes forming a nest shape, then inner cross.

examples.meaning_mm: ကန်ဆိုင် ဒေသ

description: Means 'mouth' or 'opening'. Also used for exits/entrances like 出口 (deguchi - exit) and 入口 (iriguchi - entrance). A very common radical.
meaning_mm: ပါးစပ်၊ အပေါက်
mnemonic: A simple open square — like a mouth seen head-on, just a box for speaking.
radical: Radical: 口 (mouth) — the mouth radical, extremely common in Japanese kanji.
stroke_hint: Left vertical down, then top horizontal right, then right vertical down, then close with bottom stroke.

description: Means 'eye'. Also used as a counter suffix for ordinal numbers (e.g., 一つ目 - hitotsu me, the first one). Found in words like 目的 (mokuteki - purpose).
mnemonic: A rectangle with two horizontal lines inside — an eye drawn frontally with pupils visible.
radical: Radical: 目 (eye) — the eye radical, found in vision-related kanji.
stroke_hint: Outer box first, then two horizontal interior lines.

examples.meaning_mm: အာရုံစိုက်ခြင်း

description: Means 'ear'. Used in words like 耳鼻科 (jibika - ear, nose, and throat department). Also used metaphorically in expressions about hearing news.
mnemonic: Like a stylized ear — a box shape with small horizontal ledges resembling an ear canal.
radical: Radical: 耳 (ear) — the ear radical, found in hearing-related kanji.
stroke_hint: Left vertical, top horizontal, right vertical, then two interior horizontal strokes, then bottom horizontal extending.

description: Means 'hand'. An extremely common kanji used in many compound words like 手紙 (tegami - letter), 上手 (jouzu - skillful), and 手伝う (tetsudau - to help).
mnemonic: Three horizontal lines (fingers) crossed by a vertical with a hook (palm and wrist) — a stylized hand.
radical: Radical: 手 (hand) — the hand radical, found in action-related kanji.
stroke_hint: Three horizontal strokes (top to bottom), then a vertical with a slight hook at the bottom.

description: Means 'foot', 'leg', or 'to be sufficient'. Used in words like 遠足 (ensoku - excursion) and 足りる (tariru - to be enough).
mnemonic: A mouth-like shape above foot toes — like a foot with the ankle joint (口) over the toes.
radical: Radical: 足 (foot/leg) — the foot radical.
stroke_hint: Write 口 on top, then the lower leg strokes making two diverging lines from a central drop.

examples.meaning_mm: လုံလောက်သည်

description: Means 'to enter' or 'to put in'. Used in the verb 入る (hairu - to enter) and 入れる (ireru - to put in). Part of the word 入口 (iriguchi - entrance).
kunyomi: い.る, はい.る, い.れる
meaning_mm: ဝင်သည်၊ ထည့်သည်
mnemonic: An upside-down V — like someone ducking through a small doorway, both legs visible.
radical: Radical: 入 (enter) — the enter radical.
stroke_hint: Left short stroke going down-right, then right longer stroke going down-left, crossing at top.

description: Means 'to exit', 'to leave', or 'to go out'. Used in 出る (deru - to go out), 出す (dasu - to take out), and 出口 (deguchi - exit).
meaning: to exit, to go out, to take out
meaning_mm: ထွက်သည်၊ ထုတ်သည်
mnemonic: Two mountains (山) rising from a base — like bursting up and out of something.
radical: Radical: 山 (mountain) variant — two mountain peaks rising upward and outward.
stroke_hint: Short center vertical, then taller outer vertical, cross horizontal, then second mountain shape below.

description: Means 'to stand'. Used in verbs like 立つ (tatsu - to stand up) and compound words like 国立 (kokuritsu - national). Also read as 'ritsu' in formal words.
mnemonic: A person standing tall on a ground line — like a stick figure with arms slightly out, upright.
radical: Radical: 立 (stand) — the standing radical.
stroke_hint: Top dot, then horizontal stroke, then two diagonal legs from center, then base horizontal.

description: Means 'to rest' or 'holiday'. Composed of the radicals for 'person' (亻) and 'tree' (木), suggesting a person resting under a tree. Used in 休み (yasumi - holiday/break).
meaning_mm: အနားယူသည်၊ ပိတ်ရက်
mnemonic: A person (亻) leaning against a tree (木) — taking a well-deserved rest in the shade.
radical: Radical: 亻 (person) + 木 (tree) — a person resting at a tree.
stroke_hint: Person radical on left (two strokes), then tree (木) on right.

examples.meaning_mm: ရုံးပိတ်ရက်

description: Means 'to read'. Used in the verb 読む (yomu - to read). The 'on' reading 'doku' appears in words like 読書 (dokusho - reading books).
onyomi: ドク, トク, トウ
mnemonic: Speech/words (言) combined with a selling figure (売) — 'selling your thoughts' through reading words.
radical: Radical: 言 (speech) — on the left, the speaking/words radical.
stroke_hint: Write 言 on left, then 売 (to sell) on right.

examples.meaning_mm: စာဖတ်ခြင်း

description: Means 'to write' or 'book/document'. Used in 書く (kaku - to write), 辞書 (jisho - dictionary), and 書類 (shorui - documents).
meaning_mm: ရေးသည်၊ စာအုပ်
mnemonic: A brush at the top writing on a surface below — like a calligraphy brush creating characters on parchment.
radical: Radical: 聿 (brush/writing instrument) — a brush in motion.
stroke_hint: Top brush shape (聿 — three strokes), then 者 shape below representing the written surface.

examples.meaning_mm: သင်ခန်းစာ စာအုပ်

description: Means 'to listen', 'to hear', or 'to ask'. Used in 聞く (kiku - to listen/ask) and 新聞 (shinbun - newspaper, literally 'new things heard').
meaning: to hear, to listen, to ask
meaning_mm: နားထောင်သည်၊ မေးသည်
mnemonic: An ear (耳) pressed against a gate (門) — eavesdropping at the entrance, straining to hear.
radical: Radical: 門 (gate) + 耳 (ear) — ear inside a gate, listening through it.
stroke_hint: Write 門 outer frame, then 耳 inside the gate frame.

examples.meaning_mm: ကြားသည်၊ နားထောင်သည်

description: Means 'to speak' or 'story/talk'. Used in 話す (hanasu - to speak), 電話 (denwa - telephone), and 会話 (kaiwa - conversation).
meaning_mm: ပြောသည်၊ ပုံပြင်
mnemonic: Words (言) combined with a tongue (舌) — speaking uses both your words and your tongue.
radical: Radical: 言 (speech) — on the left, the speech radical.
stroke_hint: Write 言 on left, then 舌 (tongue — 千 over 口) on right.

examples.meaning_mm: ပြောဆိုသည်

description: Means 'to say' or 'word'. Used in 言う (iu - to say), 言葉 (kotoba - word/language), and formal compounds with the reading 'gen' or 'gon'.
mnemonic: Like a mouth with multiple lines of speech flowing outward — words streaming from an open mouth.
radical: Radical: 言 (speech/say) — the speech radical, one of the most common.
stroke_hint: Top horizontal dot, then horizontal, then mouth box (口) below with two horizontal interior lines.

description: Means 'name' or 'famous'. Used in 名前 (namae - name), 名刺 (meishi - business card), and 有名 (yuumei - famous).
mnemonic: Evening (夕) above a mouth (口) — calling someone's name in the dark when you can't see their face.
radical: Radical: 夕 (evening) + 口 (mouth) — calling a name in the evening.
stroke_hint: Evening stroke (夕 — two diagonal strokes with a dot), then mouth box (口) below.

examples.meaning_mm: ဆက်သွယ်ရေးကဒ်

description: Means 'before', 'in front of', or 'previous'. Used in 名前 (namae - name), 前 (mae - in front), and 午前 (gozen - morning/AM).
meaning_mm: အရှေ့၊ မတိုင်ခင်
mnemonic: A boat stopped before a sword — like standing at the front, ready with a weapon.
radical: Radical: 刀 (sword) + 月 (moon/boat) — a sword in front stopping movement.
stroke_hint: Top part (羊 variant), then 月 on lower left, then 刀 on lower right.

description: Means 'after', 'behind', or 'later'. Read as 'ato' for 'after' (後で - atode), 'ushiro' for 'behind', and 'go' in words like 午後 (gogo - afternoon/PM).
kunyomi: うし.ろ, あと, のち
mnemonic: A person (彳) walking with tiny steps and falling behind — someone lagging to the rear.
radical: Radical: 彳 (step/walk) — the walking radical on the left.
stroke_hint: 彳 on left (three strokes), then 幺 (small/tiny), then 夂 (going slowly) on right.

examples.meaning_mm: နေ့လည်ပိုင်း

description: Means 'between', 'interval', or 'space'. Read as 'aida' for 'between', 'kan' in 時間 (jikan - time), and 'ma' for 'room' or 'space'.
mnemonic: Sunlight (日) streaming through the crack of a gate (門) — the gap or interval between two panels.
radical: Radical: 門 (gate) + 日 (sun) — sun in the gap of a gate.
stroke_hint: Write 門 outer frame first, then 日 (sun box) inside.

description: Means 'outside' or 'foreign'. Read as 'soto' for 'outside' and 'gai' in words like 外国 (gaikoku - foreign country) and 外国人 (gaikokujin - foreigner).
kunyomi: そと, ほか, はず.す
meaning_mm: အပြင်၊ နိုင်ငံခြား
mnemonic: Evening (夕) with a divination mark (卜) — going outside at evening to read the stars.
radical: Radical: 夕 (evening) + 卜 (divination) — stepping outside under the evening sky.
stroke_hint: Evening strokes (夕) on left, then divination strokes (卜 — vertical and short horizontal) on right.

description: Means 'country' or 'nation'. Used in 外国 (gaikoku - foreign country), 中国 (Chuugoku - China), and 国語 (kokugo - national language).
mnemonic: A precious jewel (玉) enclosed within borders (囗) — a treasured gem protected by a nation's walls.
radical: Radical: 囗 (enclosure) + 玉 (jewel) — a jewel enclosed, symbolizing a protected nation.
stroke_hint: Outer box (囗) first, then 玉 (king 王 with a dot) inside, then close bottom of box.

examples.meaning_mm: ဂျပန်ဘာသာ (ကျောင်းဘာသာ)

description: Means 'heaven' or 'sky'. Used in 天気 (tenki - weather), 天国 (tengoku - heaven), and 天才 (tensai - genius).
mnemonic: A big (大) person with a line above their head — the sky above all people, the great heavens.
radical: Radical: 大 (big) + 一 (one line) — the greatest thing of all: the sky.
stroke_hint: Top horizontal stroke first, then the big (大) shape: horizontal through middle and two diagonal legs.

description: Means 'spirit', 'energy', or 'feeling'. Found in many everyday words: 天気 (tenki - weather), 元気 (genki - energetic), 気持ち (kimochi - feeling), and 気をつけて (ki wo tsukete - be careful).
meaning_mm: စိတ်ဓာတ်၊ စွမ်းအင်၊ ခံစားချက်
mnemonic: Steam lines (气) with rice/energy (米 variant) — rice cooking, steam rising: the essence of energy and spirit.
radical: Radical: 气 (air/steam) — the air/breath radical surrounding inner energy.
stroke_hint: Top left stroke, then middle horizontal, then curved right stroke for 气, then 米 strokes inside.

examples.meaning_mm: ကျန်းမာ တက်ကြွသော

description: Means 'rain'. Used in weather expressions like 雨が降る (ame ga furu - it rains). Also a radical found in other weather kanji like 雪 (yuki - snow) and 雲 (kumo - cloud).
mnemonic: A horizontal roof with four drops hanging below — like rain clouds with drops falling from a canopy.
radical: Radical: 雨 (rain) — the rain radical, found in all weather-related kanji.
stroke_hint: Top horizontal stroke, then vertical, then horizontal to form roof, then four dot strokes inside.

examples.meaning_mm: မိုးရွာသောရာသီဥတု

description: Means 'electricity'. Used in many modern words: 電話 (denwa - telephone), 電車 (densha - train), 電気 (denki - electricity/light), and 電子 (denshi - electronic).
mnemonic: Rain (雨) with lightning (申) below — a lightning bolt striking in the rain, the source of electricity.
radical: Radical: 雨 (rain) — rain on top, from which lightning originates.
stroke_hint: Write 雨 on top, then 申 (rice paddy with a vertical — lightning bolt) below.

description: Means 'car', 'vehicle', or 'wheel'. Used in 電車 (densha - train), 自転車 (jitensha - bicycle), and 車 (kuruma - car).
mnemonic: A wheel viewed from above — the central axle and crossbars of a cart wheel.
radical: Radical: 車 (vehicle/wheel) — the vehicle radical.
stroke_hint: Top horizontal, then vertical down through center, then middle horizontal, then bottom horizontal.

description: Means 'road', 'path', or 'way'. Used in 道 (michi - road) and in philosophical concepts like 道 (dou/michi - the way). Part of 北海道 (Hokkaidou).
mnemonic: A head/neck (首) with a movement radical (辶) — going forward with your head up, following a path.
radical: Radical: 辶 (movement/road) + 首 (neck/head) — head moving along a road.
stroke_hint: 首 (neck) strokes first, then 辶 (movement radical) wrapping around the bottom.

description: Means 'shop' or 'store'. Read as 'mise' when used alone and 'ten' in compounds like 売店 (baiten - kiosk) and 書店 (shoten - bookstore).
mnemonic: A roof (广) over divination marks (占) — like predicting the best spot under cover to set up a market stall.
radical: Radical: 广 (roof/canopy) + 占 (occupy/divine) — occupying a spot under a roof for business.
stroke_hint: Roof (广) stroke first, then 占 (卜 + 口) inside.

examples.meaning_mm: စာအုပ်ဆိုင်

description: Means 'flower'. Used in words like 花見 (hanami - cherry blossom viewing), 花火 (hanabi - fireworks), and 花瓶 (kabin - vase).
mnemonic: Grass (艹) above a changing figure (化) — a bud transforming and blooming into a flower.
radical: Radical: 艹 (grass/plant) — plants on top, signifying flora.
stroke_hint: Two grass strokes on top (艹), then 化 (changing person) below on right.

examples.meaning_mm: ချယ်ရီပန်း ကြည့်ခြင်း

description: Means 'white'. Used as an adjective 白い (shiroi - white) and in compound words like 白紙 (hakushi - blank paper). Also a common surname component.
mnemonic: A sun (日) with an extra bright stroke on top — sunlight so intense it bleaches everything pure white.
radical: Radical: 白 (white) — the white radical.
stroke_hint: Top short vertical/dot stroke, then the sun box shape (日 — outer box with inner horizontal).

description: Means 'long' or 'leader/chief'. Used as an adjective 長い (nagai - long) and in titles like 社長 (shachou - company president) and 校長 (kouchou - principal).
mnemonic: Like a person with very long flowing hair — the village elder with the longest hair is the leader.
radical: Radical: 長 (long) — the long radical.
stroke_hint: Top strokes form the hair flowing outward, then a central vertical with crossing strokes.

description: Means 'old' (for things, not people). Used as an adjective 古い (furui - old) and in words like 中古 (chuuko - secondhand). The opposite of 新 (atarashii - new).
mnemonic: Ten (十) generations of mouths (口) passing down the story — so many mouths have told this tale, it's ancient.
radical: Radical: 十 (ten) + 口 (mouth) — ten mouths = ten generations = old.
stroke_hint: Cross (十) on top, then mouth box (口) below.

description: Means 'many' or 'much'. Used as an adjective 多い (ooi - many) and in words like 多分 (tabun - perhaps) and 多数 (tasuu - majority).
mnemonic: Two evening moons (夕夕) stacked — so many nights have passed, it means 'many.'
radical: Radical: 夕 (evening) doubled — two evening marks suggesting abundance.
stroke_hint: Upper 夕 (two strokes with dot), then lower 夕 slightly offset.

examples.meaning_mm: ဖြစ်နိုင်သည်

description: Means 'few', 'little', or 'a small amount'. Used as an adjective 少ない (sukunai - few) and in words like 少し (sukoshi - a little) and 少年 (shounen - boy).
mnemonic: Small (小) with an extra break stroke — even smaller than small, just a tiny little bit.
radical: Radical: 小 (small) + extra diagonal — diminished further, just a few.
stroke_hint: Short left stroke, then diagonal stroke, then right-falling stroke from top.

description: Means 'to meet' or 'meeting/society'. Used in 会う (au - to meet), 会社 (kaisha - company), 会議 (kaigi - meeting), and 会話 (kaiwa - conversation).
meaning_mm: တွေ့ဆုံသည်၊ အစည်းအဝေး
mnemonic: A roof with people gathering underneath — people coming together under a shelter to meet.
radical: Radical: 亼 (gather) + 人 (person) — multiple people assembling.
stroke_hint: Top inverted-V (roof shape), then two horizontal strokes, then 人 (two diagonal strokes) below.

description: Means 'company' or 'shrine'. Used in 会社 (kaisha - company), 社長 (shachou - president), and 神社 (jinja - shrine).
meaning_mm: ကုမ္ပဏီ၊ နတ်ကွန်း
mnemonic: A sacred altar (示) on earthen ground (土) — a place of worship or gathering built on soil, like a company or shrine.
radical: Radical: 示 (show/altar) — the altar radical on the left.
stroke_hint: 示 (altar: top horizontal, vertical, then three short strokes) on left, then 土 on right.

description: Means 'sky' or 'empty'. Read as 'sora' for 'sky', 'kuu' in 空港 (kuukou - airport), and 'kara' in 空 (kara - empty). Also used in 空気 (kuuki - air).
kunyomi: そら, あ.く, から
meaning_mm: ကောင်းကင်၊ အလွတ်
mnemonic: A hole (穴) that work (工) has made — like drilling through something until it's hollow: empty sky.
radical: Radical: 穴 (hole/cave) + 工 (work) — work carving out a hollow space.
stroke_hint: Roof strokes (宀), then 八 inside (穴 shape), then 工 (three strokes) below.
